judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with strong revenue expansion and a significant earnings beat, but trades at a high valuation and shows weak profitability and returns.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat consensus by 242.4%; next earnings date 2026-04-28.
positives: Revenue growth 130.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Last quarter EPS surprise 242.4% — significant beat on earnings.
Analyst growth est, current FY 179.5% — strong future growth projected.
Current ratio 5.03 and Quick ratio 3.98 — strong short-term liquidity.
Free cash flow (TTM) $265.52M — positive cash generation.
negatives: Forward P/E 71.48x — valuation is very stretched.
Profit margin 0.2% and Return on equity 1.3% — profitability and returns are very weak.
Mean analyst target $245.13 is below the current price of $299.79.
Beta 3.83 — stock exhibits very high volatility.

news.paragraph: Bloom Energy (BE) is being highlighted as a potential top climate change stock, with recent analyst reports and discussions focusing on its position in the sector. While the company has shown significant revenue growth and a strong positive surprise on its last reported earnings, the forward P/E remains high and the mean analyst target suggests a potential downside from current levels. Traders should consider the high beta and the divergence between analyst sentiment and price targets.

headlines.title: Is Bloom Energy (BE) One of the Best Climate Change Stocks to Buy According to Analysts?
